texto: I do not think that in those early days of September, Hitler was fully aware that he had irrevocably unleashed a world war. He had merely meant to move one step further. To be sure, he was ready to accept the risk associated with that step, just as he had been a year before during the Czech crisis; but he had prepared himself only for the risk, not really for the great war.
explicacao: Citação de Albert Speer sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “I do not think that in those early days of September, Hitler was fully aware that he had irrevoca…” articula reflexao, history, hitler em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como hitler e war.

frase_original: I do not think that in those early days of September, Hitler was fully aware that he had irrevocably unleashed a world war. He had merely meant to move one step further. To be sure, he was ready to accept the risk associated with that step, just as he had been a year before during the Czech crisis; but he had prepared himself only for the risk, not really for the great war.

texto: For the commission to do a great building, I would have sold my soul like Faust. Now I had found my Mephistopheles. He seemed no less engaging than Goethe's.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Albert Speer: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “For the commission to do a great building, I would have sold my soul like Faust. Now I had found …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, history, nuremberg-trials pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de nuremberg-trials e world-war-2.
